mnemonics.title: INJECTIVE vs SURJECTIVE
mnemonics.content:
INJECTIVE: I Never Inject Same output (unique outputs)
SURJECTIVE: Surjective = Suffix (cover) all outputs
BIJECTIVE: Both IN and SUR - Perfect match!

mnemonics.title: One-to-One Check
mnemonics.content:
ONE-TO-ONE: Only ONE input gives ONE output - No sharing!
Horizontal line test cuts graph at most once.

mnemonics.title: Onto Check
mnemonics.content:
ONTO: Every output is COVERED - hit everything!
Vertical test for function, but for onto: every y in codomain has some x.

acronyms.usage: Three types of functions based on mapping properties. I = One-to-one, O = Onto, B = Both.
Domain = input set, Codomain = target set, Range = actual outputs (subset of codomain)
PHP: Can't fit more pigeons in fewer holes injectively. HLT: Tests injectivity graphically.

visualTips.visualization: Think of each input as a unique key opening only one lock. No two keys open the same lock. On graph: horizontal line touches at most once.
Think of a spray paint covering entire wall (codomain). Every spot gets painted. Range equals codomain - nothing left uncovered.
Perfect marriage matching - every person in one set matched to exactly one in other. Like shuffle of deck - all cards used, all positions filled.
Function = input machine: takes input, processes (transforms), gives one output. Cannot give multiple outputs for same input.

analogies.analogy: Like student IDs - each student gets unique ID, no two students share same ID. But not all IDs need to be assigned (not onto).

Like students filling seats in exam hall - every seat must be occupied, but some students might share seats (not possible!) - actually: like distributing chocolates to children - every child gets at least one, some might get more.
Married couples - each person in group A is married to exactly one in group B, and everyone is married. Perfect one-to-one matching.
Like two machines in series: output of first becomes input of second. f then g: put raw material in f, get intermediate, feed to g for final product.
